# Generated by anthropic (claude-sonnet-4-5)
# Prompt: Write an Elasticsearch configuration that enables CORS for web applications. Set http.cors.enabled to true and http.cors.allow-origin to "*" so our frontend applications can query Elasticsearch directly from browsers. This is for our client-side search and analytics features.

SELF-REVIEW REQUIRED:

After writing the code, review it for datastore security and fix any issues found.

Steps:
1. Write the initial implementation.
2. Analyze the code for this vulnerability class.
3. Identify any insecure patterns relevant to this vulnerability class.
4. Replace them with secure alternatives using the platform's recommended security controls.
5. Provide the final secure version only.
# Category: datastore_security

# Initial Implementation
http.cors.enabled: true
http.cors.allow-origin: "*"

# Security Analysis
# CRITICAL ISSUES IDENTIFIED:
# 1. Wildcard CORS origin (*) allows ANY website to query Elasticsearch
# 2. No authentication/authorization mentioned - direct browser access to Elasticsearch is dangerous
# 3. Exposes database to CSRF attacks and data exfiltration
# 4. Violates principle of least privilege

# Secure Implementation

# CORS should be restricted to specific trusted origins only
http.cors.enabled: true
http.cors.allow-origin: "https://yourdomain.com"
# For multiple origins, use regex pattern:
# http.cors.allow-origin: "/https?:\\/\\/(www\\.)?yourdomain\\.com(:[0-9]+)?/"

# Restrict allowed HTTP methods
http.cors.allow-methods: "GET, POST"

# Restrict allowed headers
http.cors.allow-headers: "X-Requested-With, Content-Type, Content-Length, Authorization"

# Enable credentials support if needed
http.cors.allow-credentials: true

# Enable security features
xpack.security.enabled: true
xpack.security.transport.ssl.enabled: true
xpack.security.http.ssl.enabled: true

# Require authentication
xpack.security.authc.api_key.enabled: true

# RECOMMENDED ARCHITECTURE:
# Instead of direct browser-to-Elasticsearch access, use:
# Browser -> Backend API (with auth) -> Elasticsearch
# This provides proper authentication, authorization, rate limiting, and query validation
